conditions.critical_condition_basis: disappearance of the retention volume versus macromolecule size dependence

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: These conditions are optimum for the separation of PEO according to the types of terminal groups (Fig. 3). As was to be expected, for the reversed phase the retention of macromolecules with the aliphatic end RO is stronger than that with a double bond.

conditions.critical_condition_basis: disappearance of the retention volume versus macromolecule size dependence

field_evidence.critical_condition_basis: Under these conditions the unmodified sites of the reversed phase, containing-SiOH groups, become significant. Because of the extremely strong interaction of PEO with these sites, the chromatograms, even for the PEO homopolymer, are strongly distorted by elongated tails.
